reforms.description: The proposed creation of individual savings accounts aims to boost personal saving, particularly for low-income groups, through government transfers in the form of a matching income-related credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative encourages savings which can enhance financial stability and investment in human capital.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not a structural overhaul but rather an enhancement of existing savings mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The proposed creation of individual savings accounts represents an innovation that could boost personal saving in the future, particularly for low-income groups.

reforms.description: The United States signed the international Kyoto Protocol to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, committing to a budget for climate change initiatives and research on alternative energy sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in climate change research and alternative energy sources is expected to foster innovation and economic growth in sustainable technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The commitment to the Kyoto Protocol has been made but specific initiatives are still in the planning stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to addressing climate change through dedicated research funding.
reforms.source_quote: In November 1998, the United States signed the international pact negotiated in Kyoto the year before to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to 7 per cent below 1990 levels in 2008-12.
